title: Everyday Slang
description: The casual Korean that real people text and say to friends every day - the stuff textbooks skip.
situation: Hanging out with friends: reacting, hyping things up, complaining, and sounding natural rather than stiff.

phrases.tip: The all-purpose hype word - good for amazing news OR shocking news. Literally 'jackpot'.
A gasp turned into a word - reacting to something shocking, weird, or unbelievable. Very common in texts.
Stick it on anything: 너 짱이야 (neo jjangiya) means 'you're the best'. Slightly older slang but still everywhere.
Mashup of 꿀 (honey) + 재미 (fun) = 'honey-fun'. The opposite is 노잼 (nojaem), 'no fun / boring'.

phrases.english: You got this! / Go for it!
phrases.tip: From English 'fighting' but means encouragement, not violence. Shout it before exams, games, anything tough.
Filler and intensifier in one. 진짜? on its own = 'for real?'. Used constantly in casual speech.
Stack it before adjectives for emphasis: 완전 맛있어 (wanjeon masisseo), 'it's totally delicious'.
The classic shrug answer. 'Why?' '그냥.' = 'just because'. Also a soft filler word.
Very casual - only with close friends or people younger than you. Rude to elders or strangers.
Agreeing enthusiastically. Doubling it up adds energy, like nodding hard while someone talks.
꿀 (honey) + tip = a genuinely useful tip. Common in videos and posts sharing handy advice.

phrases.english: Heart-fluttering / so cute it gets me
phrases.tip: 심장 (heart) + 쿵 (thump) - that heart-skip when something or someone is adorable or charming.
English 'no' + 재미 (fun). The flat verdict on a dull movie, party, or person. Pairs with 꿀잼 as its opposite.
Literally 'so hard I could die' - playful overstatement for being worn out, not a real complaint about life.
A stretched-out, hyped version of 가자 (gaja, 'let's go'). Born online - shout it when you're pumped.

phrases.english: A sudden reality check / deflated feeling
phrases.tip: Short for 현실 자각 타임 ('reality-realisation time') - that hollow moment when reality crashes back in after a high.
